SMAD 1.27.4
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • 1. Introduction to SMAD
    • 2. Scoring Functions in SMAD

SAINTexpress-int

Source: R/SAINTexpress.R
SAINTexpress_int.Rd

SAINTexpress-int SAINTexpress scoring using intensity data (int mode). Implements the SAINT-MRF model with Normal distribution for intensity-based AP-MS data. Intensities are log-transformed and z-score normalized internally.

SAINTexpress_int(
  inter_df,
  prey_df,
  bait_df,
  GO_df = NULL,
  f = 0,
  R = 100L,
  L = 100L
)

Arguments

inter_df

A data frame with columns: ip_id, bait_id, prey_id, quant. Each row represents one interaction observation (raw intensity, NOT logged).

prey_df

A data frame with columns: prey_id. Optional columns: prey_length, gene_name.

bait_df

A data frame with columns: ip_id, bait_id, test_ctrl. test_ctrl must be "T" (test) or "C" (control).

GO_df

Optional data frame with columns: go_term, genes. genes should be space-separated gene names.

f

Frequency parameter (0 <= f < 1). Default 0.

R

Maximum number of replicates for scoring. Default 100.

L

Maximum number of controls per prey. Default 100.

Value

A data frame with columns: Bait, Prey, PreyGene, Intensity, IntensitySum, AvgIntensity, NumReplicates, ctrlIntensity, AvgP, MaxP, TopoAvgP, TopoMaxP, SaintScore, OddsScore, FoldChange, BFDR, boosted_by.

References

Teo G, et al. SAINTexpress: improvements and additional features in Significance Analysis of INTeractome software. J Proteomics. 2014;100:37-43.

Author

Hyungwon Choi, Damian Fermin (original SAINTexpress), Qingzhou Zhang (Rcpp integration)

Examples

bait_path <- system.file("exdata", "TIP49", "bait.dat", package = "SMAD")
prey_path <- system.file("exdata", "TIP49", "prey.dat", package = "SMAD")
inter_path <- system.file("exdata", "TIP49", "inter.dat", package = "SMAD")

bait <- read.table(bait_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, 
                   col.names = c("ip_id", "bait_id", "test_ctrl"))
prey <- read.table(prey_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, 
                   col.names = c("prey_id", "prey_length"))
inter <- read.table(inter_path, sep = "\t", header = FALSE, 
                    col.names = c("ip_id", "bait_id", "prey_id", "quant"))

result <- SAINTexpress_int(inter, prey, bait)
head(result)
#>    Bait   Prey PreyGene Intensity IntensitySum AvgIntensity NumReplicates
#> 1 ACTR5  ACTR5    ACTR5         .    0.9643038    0.9643038             1
#> 2 ACTR5 RUVBL2   RUVBL2    11.956   11.9563178   11.9563178             1
#> 3 ACTR5 RUVBL1   RUVBL1     8.697    8.6972303    8.6972303             1
#> 4 ACTR5 INO80C   INO80C     5.018    5.0181908    5.0181908             1
#> 5 ACTR5  ACTR8    ACTR8     5.976    5.9756255    5.9756255             1
#> 6 ACTR5   CCT2     CCT2     3.336    3.3359918    3.3359918             1
#>                   ctrlIntensity       AvgP       MaxP   TopoAvgP   TopoMaxP
#> 1             .|.|.|.|.|.|.|.|. 0.00000000 0.00000000 0.00000000 0.00000000
#> 2     1.462|.|0.324|.|.|.|.|.|. 0.77444020 0.77444020 0.77444020 0.77444020
#> 3 0.816|0.580|.|.|.|.|.|0.580|. 0.55922882 0.55922882 0.55922882 0.55922882
#> 4             .|.|.|.|.|.|.|.|. 0.08051629 0.08051629 0.08051629 0.08051629
#> 5             .|.|.|.|.|.|.|.|. 0.11749406 0.11749406 0.11749406 0.11749406
#> 6 0.324|2.982|4.025|.|.|.|.|.|. 0.01055179 0.01055179 0.01055179 0.01055179
#>   SaintScore  OddsScore FoldChange       BFDR boosted_by
#> 1 0.00000000 -5.6421837   1.000000 0.97874778           
#> 2 0.77444020  1.2335551  17.370151 0.06311952           
#> 3 0.55922882  0.2380329  13.374220 0.13826125           
#> 4 0.08051629 -2.4353528   5.203952 0.61941539           
#> 5 0.11749406 -2.0163777   6.196829 0.55342319           
#> 6 0.01055179 -4.5408520   2.121460 0.89860014
